item.title: [178] FCC drops trove of viewer complaints over Bad Bunny's 'disgusting' Super Bowl halftime show
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has released a massive trove of viewer complaints against the musician Bad Bunny, Super Bowl broadcast partner NBC, and the NFL. | 545 pages | Source: Federal Communications Commission

item.title: [41625] Privacy Impact Assessment for the Electronic Surveillance System (ELSUR) DHS
item.description: ELSUR is owned by the ICE Office of Homeland Security Investigations (HSI). ELSUR allows ICE to capture and search for information included in applications for court orders authorizing ICE to intercept oral, wire, or electronic communications, such as telephone conversations and emails. When ICE is engaged in joint investigations with state or municipal law enforcement agencies, ICE agents may also support requests filed with state courts for electronic surveillance under the applicable state’s electronic surveillance laws. These requests will be captured in ELSUR as well. ELSUR tracks these federal and state electronic surveillance requests whether or not they are granted by the court. ELSUR also supports the sharing of this information within ICE and with other federal investigative agencies as required by Title III. | 19 pages | Source: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement

item.title: [38956] 2026-05 - A-2025-00026 - Public Prosecution Service of Canada
item.description: Records from 2022 to September 29, 2025, relating to legal agents that include the following: names of agents and law firms for each fiscal year and region and the number of hours billed, hourly rates, and total annual invoices for each; policies on the use of legal agents; briefings or statistics on their use and billing; and records related to the approval of agents as well as section 4.1 and the Schedule of the Government Contracts Regulations. | 297 pages | Source: Public Prosecution Service of Canada
